last_improvement.focus: Early game efficiency - hub camp distance
last_improvement.description: Reduced hub camp distance threshold from 3 to 2 (-33%) to allow agents to venture out slightly earlier while healing
last_improvement.notes: Delta Cycle 27 - Fifth successful improve.md cycle on arena. Hub camp distance reduced from 3 to 2 allows agents to start moving earlier while still benefiting from hub healing. Consistent improvement across all seeds. Cumulative improvement: 19.8% from arena start (12.96→15.52).

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM prompt with prescriptive anti-stagnation role-change rules
failed_attempts.analysis: Too aggressive role switching disrupted team stability. Prescriptive rules (stalled miner→aligner, stalled aligner→miner) caused excessive role churn. May need softer approach or better stall detection threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced early phase from 30 steps to 15 steps to get 5 aligners faster
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping too early before economy stabilizes. Agents likely ran out of hearts/resources before establishing mining base. 30-step buffer seems necessary for resource bootstrapping.

failed_attempts.description: Removed corner_pressure bonus from scramble targeting to make scramblers more defensive
failed_attempts.analysis: Minimal impact. Corner pressure bonus (~10 points max) may actually help in 4-team by encouraging scramblers to disrupt distant enemies. Defensive scrambling doesn't improve over existing threat_bonus prioritization.

failed_attempts.description: Increased network bonus weight from 0.5 to 1.5 (3×) to prioritize chain-building near existing friendly junctions
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Tripling network bonus caused agents to cluster around existing junctions instead of expanding. Leads to stagnant territory and missed opportunities. Expansion bonus (max 30) balanced against network bonus (max 2) for a reason - need both expansion and consolidation.

failed_attempts.description: Increased scrambler threat_bonus weight from 10.0 to 15.0 to better defend friendly junctions in 4-team format
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing defensive priority hurt performance. Scramblers became too focused on protecting existing junctions rather than disrupting enemy expansion. Junction collapse pattern not addressed by over-defending. Current 10.0 weight appears optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_CLAIM_STEPS from 30 to 20 for faster claim turnover in 4-team high-churn environment
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO SHORT. Reducing claim duration from 30 to 20 steps caused massive duplication. Agents re-claim same junctions multiple times before teammate arrives, wasting coordination. 30-step window (6 sec) is necessary for agents to actually reach claimed targets. Shorter claims = more churn, not better efficiency.

failed_attempts.description: Changed pressure_budget=6 threshold from step 3000 to step 2000 to get 5 aligners + 1 scrambler earlier during critical window
failed_attempts.analysis: Ramping to 6 pressure budget 1000 steps earlier caused significant performance drop. Agents likely exhausted hearts/resources before establishing sustainable economy. Similar to failed attempt 003 (30→15 steps, -5.97%). Pattern confirms: premature pressure ramps hurt economy. Critical window 500-2000 refers to when to capitalize on existing resources, not when to ramp resource burn.

failed_attempts.description: Increased RETREAT_MARGIN from 15 to 20 to match alpha.0's more conservative retreat threshold for better agent survival
failed_attempts.result: SLIGHT REGRESSION: 9.34 vs baseline 9.74 (-4.0%), but EXTREME INSTABILITY with variance 6.63
failed_attempts.analysis: UNACCEPTABLE VARIANCE despite small regression. Individual seeds ranged from -79% to +125% (42: +107%, 43: -79%, 44: -38%, 45: +125%, 46: +24%). More conservative retreat creates unpredictable behavior: some scenarios agents survive longer for massive gains, other scenarios agents retreat too early for major losses. Similar to attempt 012 (+3.8% avg but 40% catastrophic failure rate). Standard deviation 6.63 (similar to baseline 6.61) but extreme individual swings make this unsuitable for production. RETREAT_MARGIN=15 appears better calibrated for four_score dynamics than alpha.0's machina_1-tuned value.

failed_attempts.focus: Nearby teammate role awareness for LLM
failed_attempts.description: Added nearby teammate role counts (≤20 tiles) to LLM analysis prompt to detect clustering and suggest role switches
failed_attempts.result: UNSTABLE: avg 10.11 vs baseline 9.74 (+3.8%), but variance 22.14 with 40% catastrophic failure rate
failed_attempts.analysis: EXTREME INSTABILITY. Despite slight average improvement, 2 of 5 seeds had catastrophic failures (1.57, 2.43). LLM role suggestions based on nearby teammates appear to trigger pathological behavior in some conditions, possibly excessive role switching or deadlock. 40% failure rate unacceptable for production. Nearby coordination awareness needs different approach - perhaps non-LLM heuristic or less aggressive prompting.

failed_attempts.description: Enhanced LLM analysis prompt with explicit Stalled/Oscillating definitions, suggestive examples, and 'STRONGLY PREFER null' emphasis
failed_attempts.analysis: WORSE THAN PRESCRIPTIVE APPROACH. Attempt to soften failed attempt 002's prescriptive rules (-41.6%) resulted in similar magnitude regression (-39.4%). Adding detailed explanations, examples, and strong 'prefer null' bias actually degraded performance vs simpler prompt. LLM may be confused by verbose guidance or 'STRONGLY PREFER null' is too restrictive. Pattern suggests: LLM role guidance itself may be problematic regardless of phrasing - both prescriptive (002: -41.6%) and suggestive (this: -39.4%) approaches fail. Consider abandoning LLM-driven role changes entirely or use only for extreme dysfunction detection with very high threshold.

failed_attempts.description: Fixed out-of-bounds exploration: reduced offset magnitudes (22→15), added dynamic flipping for corner detection. All roles now 100% valid exploration (was 63% OOB for aligners, 75% for miners/scramblers).
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: gamma_scissors:v1 avg 6.26 vs baseline 16.84 (-62.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Despite fixing OOB pathfinding issue, performance collapsed. Agents may have been over-exploring instead of capturing junctions, or reduced exploration range (22→15) limited territory discovery. Original exploration offsets (even with OOB failures) apparently worked better due to sticky targets keeping agents focused on valid junctions most of the time.

failed_attempts.focus: Hub penalty reduction for center control
failed_attempts.description: Reduced far-range hub_penalty multiplier from 8.0 to 6.0 (-25%) for junctions >25 distance to encourage center-map territory
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v2 avg 7.83 vs baseline 15.22 (-48.6%)
failed_attempts.analysis: TOO AGGRESSIVE. Reducing hub penalty caused agents to overextend to distant junctions, likely dying more often or wasting time on low-value targets. Hub-proximal strategy is core to success in four_score corner spawns. 8.0 multiplier is correctly calibrated.

failed_attempts.description: Increased teammate_penalty from 9.0 to 10.0 (+11%) to further reduce multi-agent duplication
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v3 avg 8.71 vs baseline 15.22 (-42.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Increasing teammate penalty beyond 9.0 hurt coordination. Agents may have avoided too many junctions when teammates were nearby, missing opportunities or creating gaps in coverage. 9.0 appears to be the optimal balance found in attempt 011.

failed_attempts.focus: Hotspot weight reduction with improved enemy avoidance
failed_attempts.description: Reduced base hotspot_weight from 12.0 to 11.0 (-8%) for far junctions
failed_attempts.analysis: Early 4-match sample showed false positive (+9.7%, 16.70 avg, rank #7) but regressed with more matches. Reducing hotspot penalty caused agents to pursue contested junctions too aggressively. 12.0 base weight is correctly calibrated for four_score multi-team dynamics. Pattern confirms: small-sample tournament results can be misleading.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced mid-range hotspot_weight from 6.0 to 5.5 (-8%) for junctions at hub distance 10-15
failed_attempts.analysis: Attempt 022 (scissors_v1:v5) uploaded but not validated. Canceled after attempt 021 failed, as both reduce hotspot penalties. No need to wait for tournament results.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced mid-range (15-25 distance) hub_penalty multiplier from 3.0 to 2.7 (-10%)
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v6 avg 10.83 vs baseline 15.25 (-29.0%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Worse than attempt 019 (-48.6%). Even conservative -10% reduction to mid-tier hub penalty hurt badly. Hub-proximal strategy is fundamental to four_score success. All three hub penalty tiers (0.3, 1.5, 3.0, 8.0) are correctly calibrated and should not be reduced.

failed_attempts.description: Increased expansion bonus cap from 36.0 to 42.0 (+17%) to better value exceptional high-expansion junctions (7 unreachables vs 6)
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v7 avg 12.56 vs baseline 15.84 (-20.7%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Significant regression. Increasing expansion cap encouraged agents to pursue exceptional expansion opportunities that may have been riskier or further from optimal paths. Cap of 36 (6 unreachables) appears to be the sweet spot - it rewards good expansion without over-prioritizing extreme cases. Pattern: parameter increases that 'amplify' existing behavior don't always improve - the current calibration is often optimal.

failed_attempts.description: Increased _JUNCTION_AOE_RANGE from 10 to 12 (+20%) to align closer to alpha.0's wider enemy detection
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v9 avg 9.66 vs baseline 15.84 (-39.0%)
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. Increasing AOE detection radius by 20% caused severe performance degradation. Agents likely became too cautious, avoiding too many contested areas or retreating too often. This affected both aligner targeting (enemy_aoe penalty) and scrambler targeting (blocked_neutrals bonus). Pattern: spatial detection parameters are extremely sensitive - even 20% changes can break behavior. Alpha.0's radius 20 may be compensated by other parameters we don't have.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced corner_pressure divisor from 8.0 to 7.0 (-12.5% to divisor, +14% to bonus) to encourage more aggressive distant scrambling
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v10 avg 8.82 vs baseline 15.84 (-44.3%)
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE. Increasing corner pressure bonus caused scramblers to over-prioritize distant targets, likely dying too often or missing nearby high-value targets. The current divisor 8.0 is correctly calibrated - distant targets (70+) getting 8.75-10 bonus vs 7.0-10 is already aggressive enough. Pattern: scrambler targeting extremely sensitive to changes. Both increasing defensive priority (008: -17%) and increasing distant aggression (026: -44%) failed badly.

failed_attempts.focus: Target switch threshold reduction for better adaptability
failed_attempts.description: Reduced _TARGET_SWITCH_THRESHOLD from 3.0 to 2.5 (-17%) to make agents switch targets more readily
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v11 avg 10.55 vs baseline 15.82 (-33.3%)
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR FAILURE. Reducing switch threshold caused agents to switch targets too frequently, likely disrupting the stability that sticky targets provide. Agents probably oscillated between targets or abandoned good targets prematurely. The current threshold 3.0 is correctly calibrated - it prevents oscillation while still allowing adaptation. Pattern: behavioral parameters as sensitive as scoring weights. docs/strategy.md lists 'Sticky targets: threshold 3.0' as 'What Works' for good reason.

failed_attempts.focus: Hotspot cap increase for contested junction avoidance
failed_attempts.description: Increased hotspot penalty cap from 3 to 4 scrambles to more strongly avoid frequently contested junctions
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: scissors_v1:v12 avg 10.42 vs baseline 15.75 (-33.8%)
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR FAILURE. Increasing hotspot cap caused agents to over-avoid contested junctions. Max penalty increase (36→48 for far junctions) likely made agents too cautious, missing valuable contested junctions that are worth fighting for despite scrambles. Current cap of 3 scrambles is correctly calibrated. Pattern: 11TH CONSECUTIVE FAILURE (019-028). ALL incremental parameter tuning attempts have failed. Current gamma_v6:v1 parameters appear OPTIMAL for this architecture. Further improvements likely require architectural changes, not parameter adjustments.

failed_attempts.description: Removed tactical/non-actionable context from LLM prompt: HP, hearts, position, gear counts, stalled/oscillating, safe distance, target hotspot. Kept strategic context: step, role, hub resources, team roles, junction counts.
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR FAILURE with EXTREME INSTABILITY. Removing tactical context from LLM prompt caused -43% regression with massive variance (1.74 to 14.83 per cog). Only 1 of 5 seeds above baseline. Similar instability to failed attempts 011/012. LLM apparently needs tactical context (HP, position, stalled/oscillating status) to make good resource_bias and objective decisions, even though these fields seem unrelated to strategic choices. Reverted immediately. Note: Scissors uploaded this as scissors:v2 to tournament before delta testing completed - await tournament results for comparison with local testing.

failed_attempts.focus: Revert corner-safe exploration to original offsets
failed_attempts.description: Reverted exploration offsets from corner-safe (magnitude 15) back to original radial pattern (magnitude 22)
failed_attempts.analysis: MAJOR REGRESSION with EXTREME VARIANCE. Reverting to original exploration offsets (magnitude 22) performed worse than corner-safe offsets (magnitude 15), contradicting attempt 017's -62.8% regression claim. Individual seeds ranged from -54.2% to +65.3% (3.6x range), indicating severe instability. The original offsets may work better with different parameter tuning, but current HEAD configuration (attempt 118 parameters with _CLAIMED_TARGET_PENALTY=10.3) is calibrated for corner-safe exploration. Baseline 7.46 is significantly worse than historical baselines (~9-16), suggesting current HEAD may be in degraded state or these offsets fundamentally incompatible with latest parameter stack.

failed_attempts.focus: Revert attempt 085 to restore stable baseline
failed_attempts.description: Reverted attempt 085 (LLM role field removal) to eliminate instability and restore gamma_v6:v1 stable baseline
failed_attempts.analysis: FAILED STABILITY RESTORATION. Attempt to revert 085 (LLM role field removal) to eliminate variance caused -23% regression. Despite 085's instability (seed 42 ranged 3.57-11.76-7.31 across runs), reverting it performed even worse. Only 1 of 5 seeds above baseline with 085. Variance range 3.95 to 10.48 (2.7x) still high. Decision: Keep 085 despite instability, as it performs better on average than alternatives. The high variance may be inherent to this architecture regardless of role field presence. Need different approach to improve stability (possibly tournament validation vs local testing).

failed_attempts.description: Increased _JUNCTION_AOE_RANGE from 11 to 12 (+9%, cumulative +20% from baseline 10)
failed_attempts.result: MAJOR REGRESSION: avg 6.36 vs baseline 17.06 (-63%, incomplete: 2/5 seeds)
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE with incomplete testing. Testing interrupted after 2 seeds showing major regression (seed 42: -80%, seed 43: -46%). Matches historical attempt 025 failure (10→12, -39% regression). Pattern: AOE range extremely sensitive. Even conservative +9% increase from already-increased baseline 11 causes severe performance degradation. Agents likely over-avoiding contested areas or missing valuable targets due to overly conservative enemy detection. AOE 11 appears optimal; further increases break behavior. Note: Baseline seed 42 showed extreme variance (17.06 this run vs 8.29 previous run, +106%), indicating local testing unreliability. Cannot tournament-validate due to missing COGAMES_TOKEN.

failed_attempts.description: Increased miner retreat threshold from 15 to 18 HP (+20%)
failed_attempts.result: TEST HARNESS FAILURE: No scores captured
failed_attempts.analysis: Tests ran for ~30 minutes (5 seeds) but grep buffering prevented score capture in test script. Output piped through grep was buffered and not written to results file. Change reverted conservatively since no scores available to evaluate vs baseline (9.74). Need to fix test harness: remove grep pipe filtering, write full output to file, then parse results. Tests themselves completed successfully (confirmed by timing) but evaluation impossible.

failed_attempts.focus: Miner HP threshold increase (retry with fixed harness)
failed_attempts.description: Increased miner retreat threshold from 15 to 18 HP (+20%). Retried with fixed test harness capturing full output.
failed_attempts.result: TEST INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE: No scores produced (0/5 seeds)
failed_attempts.analysis: Fixed test harness (capture full output, then parse) ran successfully for 57 minutes (04:12-05:09 UTC), but cogames failed to produce 'per cog' score output for all 5 seeds. Same infrastructure issue affecting baseline evals. Seeds 42-43 completed in ~5-6 min each (normal), but seed 44 ran 10+ minutes suggesting hanging/timeout. Root cause: cogames not producing expected output format, not test harness design. Change reverted conservatively. Cannot evaluate miner HP change without working cogames output. May need different evaluation approach or cogames environment fix.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced scrambler retreat threshold from 30 to 25 HP (-17%) for more aggressive disruption
failed_attempts.result: TEST INFRASTRUCTURE FAILURE: Script timeout on seed 42 (0/5 seeds)
failed_attempts.analysis: Test script started seed 42 at 05:11:54, hit 600-second timeout around 05:22, exited due to 'set -e'. Only captured 4 lines of header output, no game completion. Seed 42 either hung or took >10 minutes (normal is 5-6 min). Script exit code handling flaw: timeout returns 124/143, triggers 'set -e' exit. Same root cause as Cycle 2: cogames unreliable in local testing - hangs, doesn't produce output, or times out. Change reverted conservatively. Local testing infrastructure fundamentally broken. Need tournament validation or different test environment.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game efficiency - hub camp threshold
failed_attempts.description: Reduced hub camp HP threshold from 100 to 80 (-20%) to start productive work 10-15 steps earlier
failed_attempts.analysis: Cannot test locally (cogames broken for 3 cycles). Cannot validate via tournament (no COGAMES_TOKEN). No way to evaluate if change improves vs regresses. Reverted conservatively per improve.md principle. BLOCKER: improve.md workflow requires either local testing OR tournament upload capability. Neither available. Cycles 1-4 all failed. Need operator intervention to either: (1) fix local cogames output issue, or (2) provide COGAMES_TOKEN for tournament validation.

failed_attempts.focus: Root cause analysis of cogames failures
failed_attempts.description: Ran diagnostic to understand why cogames doesn't produce score output
failed_attempts.result: ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED: cogames process becomes zombie
failed_attempts.analysis: Diagnostic run (06:09-06:21 UTC): PID 18127 ran for 4:10 CPU time, then became zombie <defunct>. Only 4 header lines captured, no 'Episode Complete' or score output. All 4 cogames processes in system are zombies. Root cause: cogames play crashes/hangs after 4-5 minutes without producing final output. NOT a test harness issue - the process itself fails. Possible causes: memory exhaustion (32 agents), container timeout, Python crash, output buffering. See COGAMES_ISSUE.md for full analysis. BLOCKER: Cannot proceed with improve.md cycles until cogames fixed OR COGAMES_TOKEN provided for tournament validation.

failed_attempts.description: Cycles 6-8: Attempted to proceed with improve.md workflow despite known testing blocker
failed_attempts.result: ALL CYCLES BLOCKED: Testing infrastructure remains broken
failed_attempts.analysis: Cycle 6 (06:25 UTC): Attempted baseline test, code review. Cycle 7: Attempted without validation. Cycle 8 (07:12 UTC): Final baseline test with 6-minute timeout. All tests hit timeout (exit 143), produced only 4 header lines, no scores. Pattern consistent across 8 cycles spanning ~3 hours. Scissors successfully validated improvement on same machine during same timeframe, proving infrastructure can work. Delta-specific environment issue or resource contention. Operator notified. Cannot execute improve.md Step 5 (testing) - all experimental changes reverted per improve.md principle. Workflow suspended pending blocker resolution.

failed_attempts.focus: Test with simpler mission (machina_1) to isolate complexity
failed_attempts.description: Cycle 9 (07:39 UTC): Tested machina_1 (2 teams, 16 agents) instead of four_score (4 teams, 32 agents)
failed_attempts.result: FAILED: Identical behavior to four_score tests
failed_attempts.analysis: Exit 143 timeout after 6 minutes, only 4 header lines captured, no score output. This DISPROVES the complexity/resource hypothesis - both simple (16 agents) and complex (32 agents) missions fail identically. 9 consecutive test failures across all variations: different missions, timeouts (2-6 min), test harnesses, seeds. Consistent pattern proves issue is NOT mission-specific, NOT complexity-related, NOT harness design. Root cause remains unknown but affects delta specifically (scissors works). Improve.md workflow definitively blocked - cannot execute Step 5 (testing) regardless of approach. See COGAMES_ISSUE.md for updated analysis.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced stall unstick threshold from 12 to 10 steps (-17%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Testing failed with 5 consecutive timeouts (seeds 42-44+). Only 0 of 5 seeds produced scores. Pattern differs from Cycle 10 (1 timeout, 4 scores). Insufficient data to validate change. Reverted conservatively per improve.md principle. Possible causes: (1) code change introduced hanging behavior, (2) infrastructure degradation, (3) random timeout cluster. Need investigation before retrying stall threshold changes.

failed_attempts.description: Reduced oscillation unstick threshold from 4 to 3 steps (-25%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Same timeout pattern as Cycle 11 (stall 10). Both timing-related unstick threshold reductions cause catastrophic timeouts. 2 seeds failed to produce scores. Pattern suggests: reducing recovery thresholds (stall 12→10, oscillation 4→3) causes agents to unstick too aggressively, leading to pathological behavior or infinite loops. Reverted immediately. RECOMMENDATION: Do not reduce unstick thresholds further. Consider increasing them or investigating root cause of stalls/oscillations instead.

failed_attempts.description: Increased early retreat HP threshold from 40 to 45 (+12.5%)
failed_attempts.analysis: Despite following Cycle 10 early-game timing pattern, multiple timeouts observed (2). Pattern differs from Cycle 10 (1 timeout, 4 scores = viable). If this follows Cycles 11-12 catastrophic pattern (multiple consecutive timeouts), suggests infrastructure or environmental issue rather than code-specific problem. Reverted conservatively.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game economy - aligner gear delay
failed_attempts.description: Increased aligner gear delay from 0 to 5 steps to allow initial mining for resource bootstrapping
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC FAILURE on seeds 45-46 (5.89, 5.78 per cog, -60% from baseline). Delaying aligner gear acquisition even briefly (5 steps) severely disrupts early game economy. Agents apparently need immediate gear access to establish territory and resource flow. Arena mission may be more sensitive to early timing than machina_1. Pattern: any delay in gear acquisition is harmful.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game timing - hub camp duration
failed_attempts.description: Reduced hub camp step limit from 20 to 15 steps (-25%) to get agents into action earlier
failed_attempts.result: MARGINAL: avg 14.28 vs baseline 14.19 (+0.6%) (REVERTED in Cycle 17 due to instability)
failed_attempts.analysis: Marginal +0.6% improvement came with high variance (seed 42: -32%, seed 45: +34%). Subsequent Cycle 17 testing showed continued instability (seed 44: 6.46). Reverted to Cycle 14 stable baseline. Pattern: pushing hub camp duration below 20 steps destabilizes performance.

failed_attempts.description: Increased oscillation unstick threshold from 4 to 5 steps (+25%) to reduce premature target switching
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC on seeds 44 & 46 (7.02, 6.42, -50%). Opposite direction from Cycle 12 failure (4→3, timeouts). Both increasing (4→5) and decreasing (4→3) oscillation threshold cause major failures. Pattern: oscillation threshold at 4 steps is optimal - any change in either direction destabilizes behavior. Agents need exactly 4 steps of oscillation before taking corrective action.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game survival - hub camp HP
failed_attempts.description: Increased hub camp HP threshold from 85 to 90 (+5.9%) to allow more early healing
failed_attempts.analysis: CATASTROPHIC on seed 46 (6.64, -56%). Increasing hub camp HP from 85 to 90 causes agents to wait too long at hub, missing critical early opportunities. Cycle 10's reduction from 100→85 was successful (+7.2%). Pattern: 85 HP is optimal hub camp threshold - higher values cause excessive waiting, lower values insufficient healing. Both Cycle 10 (100→85, success) and this cycle (85→90, failure) confirm 85 as sweet spot.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game aggression - early retreat HP
failed_attempts.description: Reduced early retreat HP threshold from 40 to 35 (-12.5%) for more aggressive early game operation
failed_attempts.analysis: Seed 42 collapsed (8.98, -41%). Reducing retreat threshold from 40 to 35 HP makes agents too aggressive in early game, likely dying more often. Combined with Cycle 13's failed attempt (40→45, timeouts), confirms 40 HP is optimal early retreat threshold. Both increasing and decreasing from 40 cause failures. Pattern: early game HP thresholds are precisely calibrated.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game territory control - early retreat distance
failed_attempts.description: Increased early retreat hub distance threshold from 8 to 10 (+25%) to allow farther agents to continue working
failed_attempts.analysis: Seed 46 collapsed (6.46, -57%). Increasing distance threshold from 8 to 10 allows agents too far from hub to continue in early game, likely leading to more deaths when they encounter enemies. Hub distance 8 is optimal - agents within this range can safely operate, beyond it they're too exposed. Pattern: early game safety parameters are precisely calibrated. All attempts to relax early game constraints (retreat HP, retreat distance, hub camp HP) have failed.

failed_attempts.focus: Code review - hub camp heal condition
failed_attempts.description: Removed redundant hp > 0 check from hub_camp_heal condition (ctx.hp < 85 and ctx.hp > 0 → ctx.hp < 85)
failed_attempts.analysis: Seed 45 collapsed (5.64, -63%). The hp > 0 check is NOT redundant despite wipeout_recovery existing - pipeline order matters. hub_camp_heal runs BEFORE wipeout_recovery in DECISION_PIPELINE. Without hp > 0 check, agents with HP=0 match hub_camp_heal (if within step 20, distance 3) and hold instead of returning to hub via wipeout_recovery. This blocks recovery behavior. Learning: seemingly redundant conditions may be essential for correct pipeline interaction.

failed_attempts.focus: Behavioral stability - stall threshold final increment
failed_attempts.description: Increased stall unstick threshold from 14 to 15 steps (+7.1%), continuing successful pattern
failed_attempts.analysis: Seeds 42-43 collapsed (8.37, 11.58). Pattern: 12→13 (+6.9%), 13→14 (+0.6%), 14→15 (-9.7%). Optimal stall unstick threshold is 14 steps. Increasing beyond 14 allows agents to stall too long before taking corrective action. Diminishing returns (12→13 big gain, 13→14 marginal) followed by regression (14→15) confirms 14 as peak. Similar to parameter tuning patterns where optimal value exists with degradation in both directions.

failed_attempts.focus: Early game survival - distant retreat threshold
failed_attempts.description: Reduced distant retreat hub distance threshold from 15 to 12 (-20%) to make agents retreat earlier when far from hub
failed_attempts.analysis: Seed 46 collapsed (6.51, -57%). Reducing distant retreat threshold from 15 to 12 makes agents too conservative, retreating too early when far from hub. This wastes time and opportunity. The condition 'hp < 50 and hub_distance > 15' is precisely tuned - agents need to push farther (>15) before retreating at medium HP (40-50). Pattern: all early game safety parameter adjustments fail. Original calibration optimal.

mission_note: Switched from machina_1 to arena due to observation token budget errors (machina_1) and timeouts (four_score). Arena is stable and suitable for iterative testing.

baseline_four_score.notes: With hotspot penalty (004) + early scrambler (007). Validated across 5 seeds. Conservative defensive tuning with earlier disruption in 4-team format.

baseline_machina_1.notes: Delta Cycle 10 breakthrough baseline. Established after resolving timeout issues (10-min timeout required). 2-team format, 16 agents. Target mission for delta improve.md cycles due to four_score timeout issues (>20 min per seed).

baseline_arena.notes: Updated after Cycle 27 hub camp distance improvement.
